selection_notes: This file records external Ubuntu 24.04 packages with direct compile-time or runtime relationships to libvips.
Binary packages built from the libvips source package itself were excluded from the dependent list and recorded separately under excluded_same_source_packages.
Only iipimage-server has an optional libvips relationship; all other listed packages below directly build against libvips and/or directly depend on libvips at runtime.

dependents.runtime_functionality_depends_on_libvips: nip2 is a spreadsheet-like graphical image manipulation tool whose core image processing and analysis functionality is implemented on top of libvips.
evidence: Ubuntu source metadata for nip2 Build-Depends on libvips-dev.
Ubuntu binary metadata for nip2 Depends on libvips42t64.
The libvips project documents nip2 as the graphical user interface for VIPS.

dependents.runtime_functionality_depends_on_libvips: Ubuntu's PhotoQt build enables the libvips backend. libvips is used for image-size probing and image loading/decoding for formats assigned to that backend.
evidence: Ubuntu source metadata for photoqt Build-Depends on libvips-dev.
Ubuntu binary metadata for photoqt Depends on libvips42t64.
The Debian packaging used by Ubuntu passes -DLIBVIPS=ON.
The PhotoQt source tree includes a dedicated libvips image loader implementation and runtime format/backend toggles.

dependents.runtime_functionality_depends_on_libvips: ruby-vips exposes libvips image processing operations to Ruby via FFI, so Ruby applications rely on libvips for image loading, transforms, thumbnails, and saving.
evidence: Ubuntu source metadata for ruby-vips Build-Depends on libvips-dev.
Ubuntu binary metadata for ruby-vips Depends on libvips-dev.
Upstream describes ruby-vips as a Ruby binding for the libvips image processing library.

dependents.runtime_functionality_depends_on_libvips: Optional only. Ubuntu does not build iipimage-server against libvips, but the package recommends libvips-tools for image-preparation workflows around the server's tiled multi-resolution image format.
evidence: Ubuntu binary metadata for iipimage-server Recommends libvips-tools.
Ubuntu source metadata for iipimage has no libvips-dev Build-Depends, so the packaged server does not directly link against libvips.
The server's documented role is serving tiled multi-resolution images, making libvips relevant as optional tooling rather than a hard runtime dependency.

excluded_same_source_packages.reason: Part of the libvips source package itself, not an external dependent.
Development package built from the libvips source package itself.
Command-line tools built from the libvips source package itself.
